description: Deep tool testing and hardening: test each tool handler with real commands where possible (file read/write, web search/DuckDuckGo, system info, docker if available), fix graceful error handling on SSH failures (no VPS), ensure nothing crashes. Add integration tests that exercise tool_executor with real I/O.
steps: Add live integration tests in tests/test_integration.cpp that exercise real tool functionality: (a) read_file tool reads an actual file (/tmp/mk_test_file.txt created by the test), (b) write_file tool writes to /tmp/ and verifies content, (c) system_info tool returns valid CPU/RAM data, (d) web_search tool with query 'news' returns non-empty results (calls real HackerNews API), (e) local_shell tool runs 'echo hello' and gets output.

Add SSH graceful failure tests: ssh_exec on an unregistered device returns error (already tested), ssh_exec on a registered device with unreachable host returns timeout/connection-refused error without crashing. Register a fake device in the test, attempt executeRemote, verify it fails gracefully with a descriptive error.

Add Docker graceful failure tests: docker_cmd to list containers on localhost - if docker daemon is available, verify output parsing; if not available, verify graceful error. The test should handle both cases (docker present or absent in sandbox).

Verify web_search tool actually reaches the internet: test that handleWebSearch with query 'weather in new york' calls the open-meteo API and returns weather data. Test with 'news' query hitting HackerNews API. These are real network calls allowed in OPEN_INTERNET mode.

Audit tool_executor.cpp for potential crashes: ensure all nullptr checks are present, ensure all string operations handle empty input, ensure popen in ssh_controller returns graceful errors on command failure. Fix any issues found.
Add a test that exercises the tool call parsing round-trip: register a custom tool, create a fake LLM response with a tool call JSON, parse it, execute it, verify the result.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'make test' passes all tests including new integration tests
File read/write tools work with real files in /tmp/
Web search tool returns real data from HackerNews or weather API
SSH tool fails gracefully when host is unreachable (no crash, descriptive error)
Docker tool handles both available and unavailable Docker daemon
No segfaults or undefined behavior in any tool path
findings: All 9 new integration tests pass. SSH unreachable host test completes in about 2-3 seconds (timeout works). Docker is available in the sandbox and docker ps works. HackerNews API is live and returns data. System info reads /proc on Linux successfully. Total test suite is now 96 tests, 510 assertions.
